name: Sidebar
summary: A responsive sidebar component with collapsible desktop navigation and slide-out mobile drawer.
detailedDescription: Sidebar provides a dual-mode navigation component that adapts between desktop and mobile experiences. On desktop, it renders a collapsible sidebar that animates between 300px expanded and 60px collapsed widths, with hover-based open/close triggers. On mobile, it transforms into a hamburger menu button that opens a full-screen drawer with slide-in animation from the left. The component uses React Context (SidebarContext) to share open/close state across subcomponents, with support for both controlled (prop-based) and uncontrolled (internal state) modes. The DesktopSidebar uses Framer Motion for smooth width animations, while the MobileSidebar uses AnimatePresence for enter/exit transitions. The mobile drawer includes a close button (X icon) positioned at top-right and displays the navigation content in a full-height, full-width overlay. The sidebar features neutral backgrounds (light gray in light mode, dark gray in dark mode) and uses Tabler Icons for menu/hamburger controls. This component is ideal for dashboards, admin panels, documentation sites, or any application requiring responsive navigation with consistent behavior across device sizes.
purpose: Provide responsive navigation that adapts to screen size
Offer collapsible sidebar for space-efficient desktop layouts
Deliver full-screen mobile navigation with smooth transitions
behavior: Desktop: Expands/collapses width on hover with smooth animation
Mobile: Shows hamburger menu that triggers full-screen drawer
Context-based state management for nested components
Supports both controlled and uncontrolled open states
Mobile drawer slides in from left with fade animation
Auto-hides desktop sidebar on mobile breakpoints
visualCharacteristics: Hamburger menu icon on mobile header
X close button in mobile drawer top-right
recommendations: Good fit for dashboards, admin panels, and data-dense application layouts.
Suitable for performance-sensitive pages that still need polished visuals.
Combine with hero and feature sections to establish clear top-level page flow.
